rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION $30,698.00 US et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Final Judgment As To Interested Party John Stanley
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 26, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Final Judgment As To Interested Party John Stanley. The People's motion is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the People is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: FLETCHER INTERNATIONAL, LTD., VS. ALPHONSE FLETCHER, JR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Allowing Substitute Service Of Order Re Debtors Appearance And Examination
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, April 26, 2018, Line 1, Plaintiff Fletcher International, Ltd.'s, Motion For Order Allowing Substitute Service Of Order Re Debtors Appearance And Examination. Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties are ordered to appear. The unopposed Motion is granted in part. "Litigants have the right to choose their abodes; they do not have the right to control who may sue or serve them by denying them physical access." (Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1387, 1393.) Here, the evidence indicates defendant/Judgment Debtor Alphonse Fletcher, Jr. has repeatedly denied physical access to plaintiffs/Judgment Creditors to prevent personal service upon him at his residence within the St. Regis Residences, a condominium building in San Francisco. Further, on many occasions when the process server has unsuccessfully attempted personal service, the building security guard has called defendant regarding the process server and/or accompanied the process server to the door of Mr. Fletcher's residence. Thus substituted service upon the building security guard is appropriate. (See, e.g., Id. at 1392-1394.) The Court therefore orders that plaintiffs may accomplish service upon defendant Alphonse Fletcher, Jr., via substitute service of the Order re Debtors' Appearance and Examination upon the security guard at the St. Regis Residences in compliance with CCP section 415.20. The Court is also inclined to grant plaintiffs' motion as to defendant/Judgment Debtor Fletcher Asset Management, Inc., but wishes to hear oral argument as to the statutory basis for accomplishing service upon this defendant via service upon defendant Alphonse Fletcher, Jr. The Court requests that plaintiffs' counsel provide a copy of this tentative ruling and the date and time of the hearing to defendant via email as well as providing notice via telephone. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SILU CHEN VS. DAVID SHEK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatoriues, And Request For Production Of Documents; And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, April 26, 2018, Line 5, PLaintiff Silu Chen's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatoriues, And Request For Production Of Documents; And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant. Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed Motion is granted. Defendant David Shek is ordered, within 30 days of the signed Order, to serve Code-compliant verified responses to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Production of Documents, to produce responsive documents, and to provide a privilege log if any documents are withheld based on a claim of privilege. Sanctions are awarded against defendant Shek in the amount of $2,000.00 and shall be paid within 30 days of the signed Order. Plaintiff Chen is to prepare the Order and present it at the hearing for signing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SANDFORD TAYLOR VS. GLOBAL EXPERIENCE SPECIALISTS, INC. A NEVADA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition And Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 26, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANT GLOBAL EXPERIENCE SPECIALISTS, INC'S. Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition And Discovery Responses. When he filed this case, pro per plaintiff Sandford Taylor provided an address in San Francisco and a telephone number where he could be reached. Defendant Global Experience Specialists, Inc. has served discovery requests that were signed for at the address plaintiff provided. Plaintiff suggests others living at the address may have signed. Defendant's counsel has also left several voicemail messages at the number plaintiff provided. Plaintiff told the judge pro tem he did not want to communicate with counsel and thus did not pick up her calls and deleted each of her voicemail messages without listening to them. Defendant now moves for terminating sanctions or, in the alternative, to compel plaintiff's deposition and discovery responses, and for monetary sanctions. The motion is GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff is to sit for his deposition on or before May 25, 2018. Plaintiff is to answer all pending discovery requests without objections on or before May 25, 2018. Plaintiff is sanctioned $1,000, which is to be paid on or before May 25, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Global is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
